title: wolfCrypt sp_521_div_21 rejects valid P-521 edge-case point
description: wolfCrypt's SP math in sp_c32.c sp_521_div_21 has a bug in its quotient-correction step. For P-521 public keys where x² ≡ -3 (mod p) (Wycheproof tc 55, flag EdgeCaseEphemeralKey), wc_ecc_import_x963 returns MP_VAL (-98) because WOLFSSL_VALIDATE_ECC_IMPORT triggers wc_ecc_check_key internally, which calls sp_521_ecc_is_point_21, which computes x² mod p incorrectly for this specific x. Root cause: at loop i=0 in sp_521_div_21, the quotient estimate is 1 too large; sp_521_norm_21 absorbs the borrow into t1[20] leaving it as -1 (int32), but the correction check tests only t1[21] (which is 0 after normalization), so the correction never fires. The final sp_521_norm_21 cannot propagate the borrow out of t1[20], so sp_521_rshift_21 sign-extends it, producing a wrong value for x² mod p. Confirmed by GDB inspection and Python simulation. Upstream wolfSSL source: wolfcrypt/src/sp_c32.c ~line 39033. Should be reported to wolfSSL. Workaround: wycheproof_ecdh.rs logs a warning and skips this vector rather than panicking.
